sutta: once on a time the exalted 1 was staying near savathi in jeta grove in another pindika spa on that occasion the raja king pasanadi of kosala had just returned from a sharon fight having been victorious and entertained his object then the raja or king turned in the direction of the park so far as the cart road went he rode in his chariot and then got down and reached the residence now at that time a number of monks were walking up and down in the open air then pasnadi the raja of kosala went towards those monks and on reaching them thus accosted them pray or please your references where now is the exalted 1 staying that arahant i long to behold that exalted 1 and they said yonder is his logic maharaja with the door shut do you go up quietly without nervousness enter the veranda cough and rattle the door bar the exalted 1 will open the door to you and you stop here to comment this buddha was a citizen of the country of oshawa and the king of kausala was fascinated sometimes people say that the buddha's father was the king that is not true buddha's father was not the king of the country king of the country was passing the king buddha's father was just a member of the warrior cast just like in this country we have these tunkus and rajas not everyone is a sultan some of the jungkooks can be poor so in the same case you have many people belonging to the warrior cast or the noble family and the buddha's father was just 1 of them so this nadi king king pasunadi he was a disciple of the buddha so uh in his old age he came to see the buddha actually there is a very similar suta to this in the majima nikaya middle length discourses and that is majima nikaya number 89 called the dhamma there is a bit longer than the description here and it's a very beautiful suitable but this 1 because uh it appears here i read it for you all to read not to understand also so you see if you want to see a manga you go to his cookie or his heart you hear the the monk surprise the kingdom he cough cough and rattle the dough bar or you knock some people they come to visit a monk without knocking without making any sound they open the door and go in it's a bit uh ill-mannered it's good madness you want to enter the cookie of a monkey make some sound cough so the raja pasanadi of kosala went up to the lodging as he was told coughed and rattled the doorbell and the exalted 1 opened the door then pasanadi the king entered the lodging fell with his head at the feet of the exalted 1 kissed his feet and stroked them with his hands and announced his name saying sir i am [Music] i stopped here for a moment it is a indian custom that when a lay person pays respect to a monk he puts his head at the feet of the monk even some sri lankans also do that and even in malaysia once in a long time sometimes meet an indian indian lay person sometimes they also do this they'll touch your your feet not with their head or respect so this king he has such great respect for the buddha he put his head now at the feet of the buddha and stroke the buddha's feet then the buddha said but maharaja seeing what significance therein do you show me this profound humility and pay such affectionate veneration to this body of mind and he said to show my gratitude sir to show my thankfulness to the exalted 1 do i show this profound humility and pay such affectionate veneration so he said to show my gratitude to show my thankfulness to the exalted 1 do i show this profound humility and pay such affectionate veneration for the exalted 1 sir is 1 who is set on the prophet on the happiness of many folk he is 1 who establishes many folk in the aryan knowledge that is conformity with the beneficial and good dharma and as much as the exalted 1 is such a 1 that is why i show my gratitude my thankfulness in this way then again sir the exalted 1 is virtuous he has the buddha virtues the aryan the goodly virtues he is possessed of the goodly virtues that is another reason for my showing this profound humility again sir the exalted 1 has for many a day been a forest dweller a haunter of forest solitudes be sorting to the solitary lodging of the forest that is another reason yet again sir the exalted 1 is well content with whatever offerings of ropes and arms food lodging and bed supply of medicines and requisites in sickness he may receive that is another reason and he is worshipful worthy of honor worthy of offerings of salutations with glass hands he is a field of merit unsurpassed for the world that sir is another reason yet again sir as regards talk that is serious fit for opening up the mind that is to say talk about few ones a 1 thing little about contentment about aloofness about seclusion putting forth energy talk about virtue concentration of mind wisdom liberation knowledge and vision of liberation such talk as this the exalted 1 gives that pleasure without pain and without stint or stinginess since the exalted 1 does this that is a further reason then again sir the exalted 1 attains that pleasure without pain and without trouble the 4 jaanas are meditative absorption which are of the higher mind a happy abiding in the same visible lifetime since the exalted 1 does this that is a further reason again sir the exalted 1 can be called his manifold previous lifetimes thus 1 birth 2 birds 3 10 a 100 up to a 100 thousand birds likewise through the diverse contracting world cycles the diverse expanding world cycles the diverse contracting and expanding world cycles remembering at that time i had such a name for such a family of such complexion so supported thus and thus experiencing wheel and wall of such and such a lifespan i thus deceasing rose up again at that time there too i had such a name because of such a family of such complexion etc thus with all detail and characteristics he can recall his many 4 previous lifetimes since the exalted 1 does this that is another reason then again sir the exalted 1 with the heavenly eye purified and surpassing death of men beholds beings deceasing and rising up again beings both mean and excellent fair and foul gone to a happy river gone to an ill rebirth according to their deeds thus these beings given to the practice of evil deeds or evil words of evil thoughts scoffing at the aryans of perverted view reaping the fruit of their perverted view these beings when body broke up beyond death throws up again in the ways the ill born the downfall in hell or those beings given to the practice of good deeds or good words or good thoughts not scoffing at the aryans but of sound view and reaping the fruit of the sound view these beings when body broke up beyond death rose up again in the happy born in the heaven world thus with the heavenly eye purified and surpassing that of man he beholds beings since the exalted 1 does this that is another reason yet again sir by the destruction of the arsavas exalted 1 in the same visible life attains the liberation by mind the liberation by wisdom himself acquiring it by his own comprehension and realizing it abides therein since the exalted 1 does so seeing this significance do i thus show this profound humility and pay such affectionate veneration to the exalted 1 well now sir we must be going we are busy folk and have much to do and the buddha said do what seems appropriate to you maharaja so pasnadi the raja of kosala rising from his seat saluted the exalted 1 and keeping his right towards him he departed that's the end of the sukta so here this is about how the kingdom had such great respect for the buddha and he gave all the reasons why he has such great respect for the buddha in the majima nikaya suika 89 is slightly different and that is a little bit more comprehensive than this 1 now we come to uh anguttanikaya
